beats.narration_text: Shalom — this is Liam, in for Bear. Today: script-writer, the skill that turns any text into a video script. Style-agnostic — the same narration can dress as Manim, Remotion, or a doodle. The renderer is never committed here.

output: Brown Blue pedagogy arc (HOOK → INSTANCE → ABSTRACTION)
Sardonic voice — spare, dry, no fabrication
style-agnostic: shot.source left open for any builder

beats.narration_text: The skill has two constitutions — Brown Blue pedagogy and Sardonic voice — one gate script, and one output folder. It reads text in, writes a script dot md and a style-agnostic beat sheet out. The shot dot source field is always null — no renderer decided here.

props.title: Script first. Style second.
files: SKILL.md — the two constitutions + gate rules

beats.narration_text: Five phases: intake — name the one insight and key case; gate-one sequencing — find the mystery gap, derive the length; narration — Sardonic voice, discovery framing; role assignment — HOOK through BOUNDARY in Brown Blue order; emit — script dot md plus the beat sheet, then run the linter. Fix every FAIL before handing off.

props.title: Intake → arc → roles → emit → gate.

phases.label: INTAKE
phases.note: one insight + key case — push back if two insights

phases.label: GATE-1
phases.note: mystery gap + derived length tier (never pad)

phases.label: NARRATE
phases.note: Sardonic voice, discovery framing, NO FABRICATION

phases.label: GATE
phases.note: td_script_gate.py — fix all FAILs before handoff

props.footerNote: Never renders, generates audio, or spends. Hand the folder to any builder downstream.

beats.narration_text: Self-demo: feed ai-1 Chapter seven — Chapter Writing — to script-writer. Name the one insight and show the first two beats.

props.runningText: reading chapter... naming one insight + key case...
output: scanning for one insight + mystery gap

beats.narration_text: One insight: fourteen green rows in log dot csv means the Chapter Writer ran — it says nothing about what those chapters contain. Key case: the first chapter opens with the exact failure phrase the book was designed to prevent. HOOK beat: the log, all green; INSTANCE beat: the chapter's second paragraph contains that phrase. Two roles assigned. Mystery established without naming the flaw first.

props.title: SCRIPT — ai-1/07-chapter-writing.md
props.code:
# Intake
One insight: log.csv all-green ≠ chapters are good
Key case: 01-the-fluency-trap.md opens with
  "in today's rapidly evolving design landscape"
  — the exact failure the book warned against
Mystery gap: success by every metric; failure
  by the only metric that matters
Length tier: Standard (4–8 beats)

# First two beats
HOOK:
  narration: "Fourteen rows. Every status field
    reads OK." → visual: log.csv all green
INSTANCE:
  narration: "The second paragraph contains
    'in today's rapidly evolving design landscape.'"
  → visual: chapter open, phrase highlighted red

beats.narration_text: Mechanism one: the script is style-agnostic by design. The same narration must be dressable as Manim, Remotion, or doodle without a rewrite. If the script commits a renderer — says 'show the equation morphing in Manim' — the builder is locked. The renderer is chosen downstream, by the builder, from the visual intent. The beat_sheet is the spec; the style is the implementation.

props.heading: Script first. Style second.
props.body: The beat_sheet.json has shot.source: null — no renderer committed. The visual_intent field says what the viewer should see; the builder decides how. One script → Manim or Remotion or doodle, no rewrite. Committing a renderer in the script is a category error: it conflates substance with implementation.
props.quote: The script is the substance; the style is applied downstream.

beats.narration_text: Mechanism two: two constitutions, one rule for conflict. Brown Blue owns the arc — HOOK, INSTANCE, ABSTRACTION, PAYOFF, BOUNDARY — because learning sequence is a pedagogical claim, not a rhetorical one. Sardonic owns the sentences, because voice register is rhetorical. When they conflict — and they will — pedagogy settles the structure, Sardonic settles the words inside.

props.heading: Two constitutions. One tiebreaker.
props.body: Brown Blue: HOOK → ≥2 INSTANCE → ABSTRACTION → PAYOFF → BOUNDARY. This order is a learning-science claim — instances must move before abstraction lands. Sardonic: spare, dry, no hand-holding. When the arc wants a warm opener and the voice wants the cold fact — the arc wins for structure, the voice wins for words.
props.quote: pedagogy owns structure, voice owns sentences.

beats.narration_text: Mechanism three: no fabrication, enforced by inline markers. If a fact is missing from the input text, the script writes bracket-VERIFY-colon inline — not an invented number, not a plausible guess. The linter catches forbidden phrases and off-pace beats, but fabrication is subtler: a confident-sounding sentence with no source is the fluency trap inside the script itself.

props.heading: Verify or mark it.
props.body: The NO FABRICATION rule has one escape: [VERIFY: …] inline. A missing number gets a marker, not a guess. A missing person gets a marker, not an invention. The linter catches phrase-level failures; fabrication requires content-level discipline. A confident-sounding sentence with no source is the fluency trap inside the script itself.
props.quote: If a needed number/fact is missing, write [VERIFY: …] inline — never invent people, quotes, data, or history.

beats.narration_text: Script-writer: style-agnostic output, the two-constitution arc, and the no-fabrication gate. The script is the substance. The builder decides the style.

artifactLines: Brown Blue arc: HOOK → INSTANCE → ABSTRACTION → BOUNDARY
Sardonic voice: spare, dry, no fabrication
script.md + linter gate — never renders or spends

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Run script-writer on a chapter you know. Before you write a single beat: name the one insight this chapter teaches. Then state the mystery gap — the fact that should predict an outcome, and the case where it doesn't. If you can't name both in one line each, the script isn't ready to start.

props.runningText: name the one insight before writing any beats
output: what is the one insight this chapter teaches?
what is the mystery gap — fact vs surprising case?
if you can't name both in one line, keep reading
the script starts after intake, not before

beats.narration_text: Claude, Scripted. I'm Liam, in for Bear.
